description: AdGuard Home REST-ish API.  Our admin web interface is built on top of this REST-ish API.

tools.name: access_list
tools.description: List (dis)allowed clients, blocked hosts, etc. Returns the AccessListResponse.

tools.name: access_set
tools.description: Set (dis)allowed clients, blocked hosts, etc. Optional: allowed_clients, blocked_hosts, disallowed_clients.

params.name: allowed_clients
params.description: The allowlist of clients: IP addresses, CIDRs, or ClientIDs.

params.name: disallowed_clients
params.description: The blocklist of clients: IP addresses, CIDRs, or ClientIDs.

tools.name: adguard-home-profile_get
tools.description: Get. Returns the ProfileInfo.

tools.name: adguard-home-profile_update
tools.description: Updates current user info. Required: language, name, theme.

tools.name: apple_mobile-config-do-h
tools.description: Get DNS over HTTPS .mobileconfig. Required: host. Optional: client_id.

params.name: host
params.description: Host for which the config is generated. If no host is provided, `tls.server_name` from the configuration file is used. If `tls.server_name` is not set, the API returns an error with a 500 status.

tools.name: apple_mobile-config-do-t
tools.description: Get DNS over TLS .mobileconfig. Required: host. Optional: client_id.

params.name: host
params.description: Host for which the config is generated. If no host is provided, `tls.server_name` from the configuration file is used. If `tls.server_name` is not set, the API returns an error with a 500 status.

tools.name: blocked-services_all
tools.description: Get available services to use for blocking. Returns the BlockedServicesAll.

tools.name: blocked-services_available-services
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `GET /blocked_services/all` instead. Returns array of string.

tools.name: blocked-services_list
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `GET /blocked_services/get` instead. Returns array of string.

tools.name: blocked-services_schedule
tools.description: Get blocked services. Returns the BlockedServicesSchedule.

tools.name: blocked-services_schedule-update
tools.description: Update blocked services. Optional: ids, schedule.

params.name: ids
params.description: The names of the blocked services.

params.name: schedule
params.description: Sets periods of inactivity for filtering blocked services. The schedule contains 7 days (Sunday to Saturday) and a time zone.

tools.name: blocked-services_set
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `PUT /blocked_services/update` instead.

tools.name: cache-clear_cache_clear
tools.description: Clear DNS cache.

tools.name: clients_add
tools.description: Add a new client. Optional: blocked_services, blocked_services_schedule, filtering_enabled (plus 14 more).

params.name: blocked_services_schedule
params.description: Sets periods of inactivity for filtering blocked services. The schedule contains 7 days (Sunday to Saturday) and a time zone.

params.name: ignore_querylog
params.description: NOTE: If `ignore_querylog` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/add` request then default value (false) will be used. If `ignore_querylog` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/update` request then the existing value will not be changed. This behaviour can be changed in the future versions.

params.name: ignore_statistics
params.description: NOTE: If `ignore_statistics` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/add` request then default value (false) will be used. If `ignore_statistics` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/update` request then the existing value will not be changed. This behaviour can be changed in the future versions.

params.name: upstreams_cache_enabled
params.description: NOTE: If `upstreams_cache_enabled` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/add` request then default value (false) will be used. If `upstreams_cache_enabled` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/update` request then the existing value will not be changed. This behaviour can be changed in the future versions.

params.name: upstreams_cache_size
params.description: NOTE: If `upstreams_cache_enabled` is not set in HTTP API `GET /clients/update` request then the existing value will not be changed. This behaviour can be changed in the future versions.

tools.name: clients_delete
tools.description: Remove a client. Optional: name.

tools.name: clients_find
tools.description: Get information about clients by their IP addresses or ClientIDs. Optional: ip0. Returns array of ClientsFindEntry.

params.name: ip0
params.description: Filter by IP address or ClientIDs. Parameters with names `ip1`, `ip2`, and so on are also accepted and interpreted as 'ip0 OR ip1 OR ip2'. TODO(a.garipov): Replace with a better query API.

tools.name: clients_search
tools.description: Retrieve information about clients by performing an exact match search using IP addresses, CIDRs, MAC addresses, or ClientIDs. Optional: clients. Returns array of ClientsFindEntry.

tools.name: clients_status
tools.description: Get information about configured clients. Returns the Clients.

tools.name: clients_update
tools.description: Update client information. Optional: data, name.

tools.name: dhcp_add-static-lease
tools.description: Adds a static lease. Required: hostname, ip, mac.

tools.name: dhcp_check-active
tools.description: Searches for an active DHCP server on the network. Optional: interface. Returns the new DhcpSearchResult.

params.name: interface
params.description: The name of the network interface

tools.name: dhcp_interfaces
tools.description: Gets the available interfaces. Returns the NetInterfaces.

tools.name: dhcp_remove-static-lease
tools.description: Removes a static lease. Required: hostname, ip, mac.

tools.name: dhcp_reset
tools.description: Reset DHCP configuration.

tools.name: dhcp_reset-leases
tools.description: Reset DHCP leases.

tools.name: dhcp_set-config
tools.description: Updates the current DHCP server configuration. Optional: enabled, interface_name, v4 (plus 1 more).

tools.name: dhcp_status
tools.description: Gets the current DHCP settings and status. Returns the DhcpStatus.

tools.name: dhcp_update-static-lease
tools.description: Updates IP address, hostname of the static lease. IP version must be the same as previous. Required: hostname, ip, mac.

tools.name: dns-config_dns_config
tools.description: Set general DNS parameters. Optional: blocked_response_ttl, blocking_ipv4, blocking_ipv6 (plus 26 more).

params.name: bootstrap_dns
params.description: Bootstrap servers, port is optional after colon. Empty value will reset it to default values.

params.name: cache_enabled
params.description: Enables or disables the DNS response cache. If `cache_enabled` is `true`, the companion field `cache_size` must be present and greater than 0, or the `dns.cache_size` setting in the configuration file must already be greater than 0.

params.name: fallback_dns
params.description: List of fallback DNS servers used when upstream DNS servers are not responding. Empty value will clear the list.

params.name: local_ptr_upstreams
params.description: Upstream servers, port is optional after colon. Empty value will reset it to default values.

params.name: protection_disabled_until
params.description: Protection is pause until this time. Nullable.

params.name: ratelimit_subnet_subnet_len_ipv4
params.description: Length of the subnet mask for IPv4 addresses.

params.name: ratelimit_subnet_subnet_len_ipv6
params.description: Length of the subnet mask for IPv6 addresses.

params.name: ratelimit_whitelist
params.description: List of IP addresses excluded from rate limiting.

params.name: upstream_dns
params.description: Upstream servers, port is optional after colon. Empty value will reset it to default values.

params.name: upstream_timeout
params.description: The number of seconds to wait for a response from the upstream server

tools.name: dns-info_dns_info
tools.description: Get general DNS parameters. Returns the DnsInfoDnsInfoResponse.

tools.name: filtering_add-url
tools.description: Add filter URL or an absolute file path. Optional: name, url, whitelist.

params.name: url
params.description: URL or an absolute path to the file containing filtering rules.

tools.name: filtering_check-host
tools.description: Check if host name is filtered. Required: name. Optional: client, qtype. Returns the FilterCheckHostResponse.

params.name: client
params.description: Optional ClientID or client IP address

tools.name: filtering_config
tools.description: Set filtering parameters. Optional: enabled, interval.

tools.name: filtering_refresh
tools.description: Reload filtering rules from URLs. This might be needed if new URL was just added and you don't want to wait for automatic refresh to kick in. This API request is ratelimited, so you can call it freely as often as you like, it wont create unnecessary burden on servers that host the URL. This should work as intended, a `force` parameter is offered as last-resort attempt to make filter lists fresh. If you ever find yourself using `force` to make something work that otherwise wont, this is a bug and report it accordingly. Optional: whitelist. Returns the new FilterRefreshResponse.

tools.name: filtering_remove-url
tools.description: Remove filter URL. Optional: url, whitelist.

params.name: url
params.description: Previously added URL containing filtering rules

tools.name: filtering_set-rules
tools.description: Set user-defined filter rules. Optional: rules.

tools.name: filtering_set-url
tools.description: Set URL parameters. Optional: data, url, whitelist.

tools.name: filtering_status
tools.description: Get filtering parameters. Returns the FilterStatus.

tools.name: i18n_change-language
tools.description: Change current language. Argument must be an ISO 639-1 two-letter code. Required: language.

params.name: language
params.description: The current language or the language to set.

tools.name: i18n_current-language
tools.description: Get currently set language. Result is ISO 639-1 two-letter code. Empty result means default language. Returns the LanguageSettings.

tools.name: install_check-config
tools.description: Checks configuration. Optional: dns, set_static_ip, web. Returns the new CheckConfigResponse.

tools.name: install_configure
tools.description: Applies the initial configuration. Required: dns, password, username, web.

tools.name: install_get-addresses
tools.description: Gets the network interfaces information. Returns the AddressesInfo.

tools.name: login_login
tools.description: Perform administrator log-in. Optional: name, password.

tools.name: logout_logout
tools.description: Perform administrator log-out.

tools.name: parental_disable
tools.description: Disable parental filtering.

tools.name: parental_enable
tools.description: Enable parental filtering.

tools.name: parental_status
tools.description: Get parental filtering status. Returns the ParentalStatusResponse.

tools.name: protection_set
tools.description: Set protection state and duration. Required: enabled. Optional: duration.

params.name: duration
params.description: Duration of a pause, in milliseconds. Enabled should be false.

tools.name: querylog_get-query-log-config
tools.description: Get query log parameters. Returns the GetQueryLogConfigResponse.

tools.name: querylog_put-query-log-config
tools.description: Set query log parameters. Required: anonymize_client_ip, enabled, ignored, interval. Optional: ignored_enabled.

params.name: ignored
params.description: List of host names, which should not be written to log

params.name: ignored_enabled
params.description: If true, the host names in the `ignored` array are excluded from the query log.

params.name: interval
params.description: Time period for query log rotation in milliseconds.

tools.name: querylog_query-log
tools.description: Get DNS server query log. Optional: older_than, offset, limit (plus 3 more). Returns array of QueryLogItem.

params.name: offset
params.description: Specify the ranking number of the first item on the page. Even though it is possible to use 'offset' and 'older_than', we recommend choosing one of them and sticking to it.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of records to be returned

params.name: search
params.description: Filter by domain name or client IP

params.name: response_status
params.description: Deprecated: Use 'reason' parameter instead Filter by response status NOTE: This parameter cannot be used with 'reason' parameter.

params.name: reason
params.description: Filter by response filtering reason. Multiple reasons can be provided. NOTE: This parameter cannot be used with 'response_status' parameter.

tools.name: querylog-clear_querylog_clear
tools.description: Clear query log.

tools.name: querylog-config_query-log-config
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `PUT /querylog/config/update` instead. Optional: anonymize_client_ip, enabled, interval.

params.name: interval
params.description: Time period for query log rotation.

tools.name: querylog-info_query-log-info
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `GET /querylog/config` instead. NOTE: If `interval` was configured by editing configuration file or new HTTP API call `PUT /querylog/config/update` and it's not equal to previous allowed enum values then it will be equal to `90` days for compatibility reasons. Returns the QueryLogConfig.

tools.name: rewrite_add
tools.description: Add a new Rewrite rule. Optional: answer, domain, enabled (default: true).

params.name: answer
params.description: value of A, AAAA or CNAME DNS record

params.name: enabled
params.description: Optional. If omitted on add, defaults to `true`. On update, omitted preserves previous value.

tools.name: rewrite_delete
tools.description: Remove a Rewrite rule. Optional: answer, domain, enabled (default: true).

params.name: answer
params.description: value of A, AAAA or CNAME DNS record

params.name: enabled
params.description: Optional. If omitted on add, defaults to `true`. On update, omitted preserves previous value.

tools.name: rewrite_list
tools.description: Get list of Rewrite rules. Returns array of RewriteEntry.

tools.name: rewrite_settings-get
tools.description: Get rewrite settings. Returns the RewriteSettings.

tools.name: rewrite_settings-update
tools.description: Update rewrite settings. Required: enabled.

tools.name: rewrite_update
tools.description: Update a Rewrite rule. Optional: target, update.

tools.name: safebrowsing_disable
tools.description: Disable safebrowsing.

tools.name: safebrowsing_enable
tools.description: Enable safebrowsing.

tools.name: safebrowsing_status
tools.description: Get safebrowsing status. Returns the SafebrowsingStatusResponse.

tools.name: safesearch_disable
tools.description: Disable safesearch.

tools.name: safesearch_enable
tools.description: Enable safesearch.

tools.name: safesearch_settings
tools.description: Update safesearch settings. Optional: bing, duckduckgo, ecosia (plus 5 more).

tools.name: safesearch_status
tools.description: Get safesearch status. Returns the SafeSearchConfig.

tools.name: stats_get-config
tools.description: Get statistics parameters. Returns the GetStatsConfigResponse.

tools.name: stats_put-config
tools.description: Set statistics parameters. Required: enabled, ignored, interval. Optional: ignored_enabled.

params.name: ignored
params.description: List of host names, which should not be counted

params.name: ignored_enabled
params.description: If true, the host names in the `ignored` array are excluded from the statistics.

tools.name: stats_stats
tools.description: Get DNS server statistics. Optional: recent. Returns the Stats.

params.name: recent
params.description: The lookback period for statistics in milliseconds. The interval must be a multiple of one hour and must not be greater than the value of `statistics.interval`.

tools.name: stats-config_stats_config
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `PUT /stats/config/update` instead. Optional: interval.

params.name: interval
params.description: Time period to keep the data. `0` means that the statistics is disabled.

tools.name: stats-info_stats_info
tools.description: Deprecated: Use `GET /stats/config` instead. NOTE: If `interval` was configured by editing configuration file or new HTTP API call `PUT /stats/config/update` and it's not equal to previous allowed enum values then it will be equal to `90` days for compatibility reasons. Returns the StatsConfig.

tools.name: stats-reset_stats_reset
tools.description: Reset all statistics to zeroes.

tools.name: status_status
tools.description: Get DNS server current status and general settings. Returns the ServerStatus.

tools.name: test-upstream-dns_test_upstream_dns
tools.description: Test upstream configuration. Required: bootstrap_dns, upstream_dns. Optional: fallback_dns, private_upstream. Returns the new UpstreamsConfigResponse.

params.name: bootstrap_dns
params.description: Bootstrap DNS servers, port is optional after colon.

params.name: fallback_dns
params.description: Fallback DNS servers, port is optional after colon.

params.name: private_upstream
params.description: Local PTR resolvers, port is optional after colon.

params.name: upstream_dns
params.description: Upstream DNS servers, port is optional after colon.

tools.name: tls_configure
tools.description: Updates current TLS configuration. Optional: certificate_chain, certificate_path, dns_names (plus 20 more). Returns the new TlsConfig.

params.name: certificate_chain
params.description: Base64 string with PEM-encoded certificates chain

params.name: dns_names
params.description: The value of SubjectAltNames field of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: issuer
params.description: The issuer of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: not_after
params.description: The NotAfter field of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: not_before
params.description: The NotBefore field of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: port_dns_over_quic
params.description: DNS-over-QUIC port. If 0, DoQ will be disabled.

params.name: port_dns_over_tls
params.description: DNS-over-TLS port. If 0, DoT will be disabled.

params.name: port_https
params.description: HTTPS port. If 0, HTTPS will be disabled.

params.name: private_key
params.description: Base64 string with PEM-encoded private key

params.name: private_key_saved
params.description: Set to true if the user has previously saved a private key as a string. This is used so that the server and the client don't have to send the private key between each other every time, which might lead to security issues.

params.name: serve_plain_dns
params.description: Set to true if plain DNS is allowed for incoming requests.

params.name: server_name
params.description: server_name is the hostname of your HTTPS/TLS server

params.name: subject
params.description: The subject of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: valid_cert
params.description: Set to true if the specified certificates chain is a valid chain of X509 certificates.

params.name: valid_chain
params.description: Set to true if the specified certificates chain is verified and issued by a known CA.

params.name: valid_key
params.description: Set to true if the key is a valid private key.

params.name: valid_pair
params.description: Set to true if both certificate and private key are correct.

params.name: warning_validation
params.description: A validation warning message with the issue description.

tools.name: tls_status
tools.description: Returns TLS configuration and its status.

tools.name: tls_validate
tools.description: Checks if the current TLS configuration is valid. Optional: certificate_chain, certificate_path, dns_names (plus 20 more). Returns the new TlsConfig.

params.name: certificate_chain
params.description: Base64 string with PEM-encoded certificates chain

params.name: dns_names
params.description: The value of SubjectAltNames field of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: issuer
params.description: The issuer of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: not_after
params.description: The NotAfter field of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: not_before
params.description: The NotBefore field of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: port_dns_over_quic
params.description: DNS-over-QUIC port. If 0, DoQ will be disabled.

params.name: port_dns_over_tls
params.description: DNS-over-TLS port. If 0, DoT will be disabled.

params.name: port_https
params.description: HTTPS port. If 0, HTTPS will be disabled.

params.name: private_key
params.description: Base64 string with PEM-encoded private key

params.name: private_key_saved
params.description: Set to true if the user has previously saved a private key as a string. This is used so that the server and the client don't have to send the private key between each other every time, which might lead to security issues.

params.name: serve_plain_dns
params.description: Set to true if plain DNS is allowed for incoming requests.

params.name: server_name
params.description: server_name is the hostname of your HTTPS/TLS server

params.name: subject
params.description: The subject of the first certificate in the chain.

params.name: valid_cert
params.description: Set to true if the specified certificates chain is a valid chain of X509 certificates.

params.name: valid_chain
params.description: Set to true if the specified certificates chain is verified and issued by a known CA.

params.name: valid_key
params.description: Set to true if the key is a valid private key.

tools.name: update_begin
tools.description: Begin auto-upgrade procedure.

tools.name: version-json_get
tools.description: Gets information about the latest available version of AdGuard. Optional: recheck_now. Returns the new VersionInfo.

params.name: recheck_now
params.description: If false, server will check for a new version data only once in several hours.
